raw_consumer_statement:
Hello, On XX/XX/2024, I lost my physical Citi Premier credit card and discovered 3 pending fraudulent/unauthorized charges on my account : 1.XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( Online Merchant ) - {$1500.00} XXXX XXXX - {$37.00} XXXX - {$6.00} On the same day XX/XX/2024, I immediately called to report my card lost and report the fraudulent charges. The old compromised card was closed and a card replacement with a new number was issued. The charges were marked for fraudulent investigation though they were pending. Officially, the fraud dispute process did not start until XX/XX/2024. Since then, I have not heard anything from Citi and the charges remain as part of my current balance with an approaching payment date to pay the amount. No temporary credit was issued. I understand that Citi bank has told me that I do not have to pay these reportedly fraudulent transactions while the investigation is happening. However, it is not fair to have these charges on my balance WITHOUT a temporary security credit. The reasons are as follows : 1. The fraudulent charges are marked under investigation but still account as part of my statement balance and an approaching payment date of XXXX XXXX XXXX  is when the balance is needed to be paid. This has the negative conseqeuence of having big balance and high utilization ratio reported to the credit bureau since they are not aware these charges are under investigation. As such, my credit score is negatively impacted for the time being. 

XXXX. Given that these fraudulent charges are still part of my card balance, it counts against the total allocated credit limit I can use. Again, I'm being penalized for acts of fraud. 

XXXX. I have never seen such a degree of negligence by a bank in my whole life. Any other bank would have issued a temporary credit while the charge is investigated. ALSO, any other bank I have used, would have verified these transactions much more thoroughly and prevented the fraud attack in the first place. Interestingly, I have not received a single notification of these charges from Citi despite one of them being valued at {$1500.00} at an online merchant I have never done business with. In many cases and given the characteristics of the transaction ( Online/large amount/new merchant ), the bank will always verify with a second factor authentication. However, Citi DID NOT attempt this and instantly approved the fraudulent transaction. This demonstrates negligence from Citi side to protect their customers. 

The Citi fraud team has been not cooperative in any attempts to listen and answer my concerns despite NUMEROUS call attempts and false reassurances.
